reforms.description: The Australian Government introduced a 150 percent tax deduction for R&D and established a Standing Committee on Tertiary Education/Industry relationships to enhance cooperation between the higher education system and industry needs, aiming to induce research and development through cooperative ventures.
reforms.component_name: R&D Tax Deduction and Industry Committee
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax deduction incentivizes private R&D investment, while the committee fosters collaboration, both of which enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax deduction and committee have been operational since their introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a significant tax incentive and the establishment of a committee represent major structural changes in the innovation policy landscape.
reforms.source_quote: A Standing Committee on Tertiary Education/Industry relationships was established in late 1985 to indicate areas where the higher education system can become more responsive to industry needs.

reforms.description: The report by EPAC highlights a significant improvement in Australia's private R&D effort, with private business enterprises increasing their annual R&D expenditure by 52 percent in real terms from 1981-82 to 1984-85.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The substantial increase in private R&D expenditure indicates a positive trend in innovation investment, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in R&D expenditure has been documented and is reflective of ongoing trends.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the increase in expenditure is a result of existing policies rather than a new reform.
reforms.source_quote: The report also shows that in recent years there has been a significant improvement in Australia's private R&D effort.

reforms.description: The government announced an eight-year program to reduce nominal tariff rates for clothing and household textile products, footwear, and fabrics, aiming to encourage a more innovative and competitive TCF sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing tariffs is expected to enhance competition and innovation within the TCF sector, leading to improved productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The program has been officially announced and is set to be implemented over eight years.
reforms.importance_rationale: The program represents a significant policy shift aimed at fostering innovation in a key industry sector.
reforms.source_quote: The Government recently announced an eight-year programme which comes into full operation in 1989 whereby the nominal tariff rate will be reduced to 60 percent for clothing and household textile products, 50 percent for footwear and 40 percent for fabrics.

reforms.description: The Heavy Engineering Adjustment and Development Programme was announced to enhance the skills and efficiency of the workforce in the heavy engineering sector through various support measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing workforce skills, the program aims to improve productivity and innovation capacity in the heavy engineering sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The program has been officially announced and is set to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The program represents a significant investment in human capital aimed at transforming the heavy engineering sector.
reforms.source_quote: In mid-1986, in response to an IAC report, the government announced a plan for the heavy engineering industry to operate for three years.

reforms.description: The Government announced an Adjustment and Development Programme for the Heavy Engineering Industry with an estimated cost of $60 million, aimed at enhancing the industry's capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The significant funding is intended to bolster the capabilities of the heavy engineering industry, contributing to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding program has been operational since its announcement.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the funding is substantial, it is part of a broader adjustment program rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Announcement on Adjustment and Development Programme for the Heavy Engineering Industry with an estimated cost of $60 million.
